summary: R2 fixes applied for systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES: M3 obligation runtime validation now covers new category/source/accounting/audit/claim/source movement fields with structured invariant errors; settlement commands enforce action-specific amount/resource semantics; deferral advances due and accounting cycle state consistently; non-fulfillment settlements now emit sim.obligation-settled and audit eventKind settled.

tests_run.result: Modified protocol/sim-core/tests/project state files from the task branch; untracked preserved review/route handoffs and tests/sim-core-m3-tribute-troop-obligations.test.ts.

R2 regression tests initially exposed three failures: audit reliabilityBps malformed-state validation missing, and duplicate terminal settlement hidden by amount validation in two duplicate/save-boundary paths.
1 test file passed; 10 tests passed after R2 fixes.
13 test files passed; 85 tests passed. Node emitted existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Node/worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 23 files/144 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.
First ad hoc shape check used stale field names title/owner/allowed_paths/forbidden_paths and failed before correction; no repository files were changed by that failed check.

decisions: Runtime validation treats malformed M3 obligation state as structured WorldInvariantError results instead of throwing, including obligationCategory, obligationSource, accounting, audit actionKind/dueDay/reasonCodes/reliabilityBps, fulfillment actionKind/dueDay, and source movement amount/headcount.

Terminal fulfillment now requires a positive amount exactly equal to the current remaining due; partial-fulfillment requires a positive underfull amount and stays non-terminal, allowing later settlement for the same due period.

Non-resource settlement actions deferral, refusal, remission, pursuit-recovery, and default-breach require fulfilledAmount 0 and do not draw debtor resources; tests assert unchanged cash and empty sourceMovements for remission/pursuit/default.
Deferral advances the obligation due state and accounting dueDay/cycle together, leaving arrears visible for the deferred period and allowing fulfillment against the new dueDay.
The domain event kind is now sim.obligation-settled and audit eventKind is settled; actionKind carries fulfillment/refusal/remission/pursuit/default detail.
Duplicate terminal settlement checks run before action amount validation so repeat due-period settlement returns duplicate-obligation-settlement consistently.
risks: R3 protocol/world-state shape changed in command/event vocabulary and invariant validation; systems_architect should review event naming and validator surface before QA.

M3 save-format support is intentionally not implemented because packages/save-format is forbidden for this task; current tests continue to assert structured unsupported behavior at the M3 save boundary.
Troop obligations remain M3 commitment/headcount/response accounting only; no M4 campaign movement, army movement, combat, or full diplomacy semantics were introduced.
requested_action: Review R2 fixes for systems findings, especially runtime state validation completeness, action-specific settlement semantics, deferral accounting consistency, and sim.obligation-settled/audit settled naming before routing onward.
